The classification of Disticholiparis merrillii is further divided into the family Orchidaceae, subfamily Epidendroideae, tribe Gastrodieae, and subtribe Gastrodiinae. intro: Disticholiparis merrillii, also known as Merrill's liparis, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions including fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Disticholiparis merrillii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Disticholiparis merrillii is often prepared in the form of a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the production of herbal teas and tonics.
